implemented_claims.summary: AS now has a named transition claim and proof-certificate entry for the recipient non-init command-message rejection boundary.

implemented_traces.summary: AS now has a schematic-linked trace that replays fixed-cell upstream standard-signal command-message rejection.

formal_command_table.summary: The formal command table includes standard-signal as a command-buffer command.

legacy_special_message_exclusion.summary: The legacy special-message sets exclude standard-signal and treat standard input as a separate classification.

standard_signal_status.as_boundary: Continue rejecting standard-signal command-message input until AS decides whether it belongs to command-message execution or standard-signal routing.

legacy_divergences.summary: RAA dispatches write-buf-zero and write-buf-one through write-buf, which appends only when the buffer-full check permits it; input processing separately clears channels.
SEMSIM defines write-buf-zero and write-buf-one as appends, but stem-process-special-message wraps the selected operation in zero-hig and zero-buf, creating an unclear buffer-clearing boundary.
FSMSIM dispatches write-buf-zero and write-buf-one through primitive-eval and appends to the buffer while clearing the self mailbox and input channels.

write_buffer_status.as_boundary: Continue rejecting write-buffer command-message input until AS selects fixed-cell, stem-cell, input-clearing, and buffer-full behavior from source evidence.

multi_command_input_status.summary: multiple simultaneous command-message inputs are not yet assigned a priority, sequencing, or rejection policy by an AS source-status decision.
multi_command_input_status.as_boundary: ADR-0049 consumes exactly one single command-message input token and leaves multiple simultaneous command-message tokens at the rejected-input boundary.

allowed_next_slices: Add a rendered SVG for the non-init command-message rejection trace.
Resolve write-buffer command-message execution semantics before appending to recipient buffers.
Resolve standard-signal command-message divergence before routing or executing standard-signal command tokens.
